title: instruction
description: Always-on or conditionally loaded behavioral context (docs/ontology/README.md §1.1, core entity 'instruction'). Non-equivalence rule from the same table: instructions guide the model; they do not enforce security. This file is the machine-readable concept declaration internal/ontology loads; it doubles as a JSON Schema for the canonical fields of one normalized instruction record, plus x- extension keys (not standard JSON Schema keywords, but valid sibling data) carrying the logical-identity rule and allowed merge operators from docs/ontology/README.md §3.1/§3.2.

order.description: Position in the CONCAT_ORDERED composition (docs/ontology/README.md §3.2: e.g. Codex 'Global then root-to-cwd CONCAT_ORDERED; closer instructions appear later').

alwaysOn.description: Distinguishes always-on base/system-prompt instructions from conditionally loaded ones (docs/ontology/README.md canonical tree, N1 vs N3/N4).

x-nonEquivalenceRule: Instructions guide the model; they do not enforce security.

x-logicalIdentity.rule: Two physical representations are the same logical instruction only when they resolve to the same scope root and the same source path. This is deliberately narrower than 'produces the same rendered text': a company CLAUDE.md and a project CLAUDE.md with identical content are still two distinct instructions (two scope roots), each contributing its own entry to the ordered composition, not a single deduplicated one. Concatenation order across distinct instructions is composition (CONCAT_ORDERED), never identity.
